description: Machine-readable organ identity definitions — domain boundaries, inclusion/exclusion criteria, canonical repo types, and boundary tests for each organ in the ORGANVM system.

ORGAN-I.name: Theoria
ORGAN-I.domain_boundary: Foundational theory, epistemological frameworks, recursive engines, ontological systems, symbolic computing. Ideas that exist as pure conceptual structures — not yet materialized into art or product.
inclusion_criteria: Produces conceptual frameworks, algorithms, or engines that other organs consume
No direct user-facing interface (consumed via ORGAN-II or ORGAN-III)
Primary output is knowledge structures, not artifacts
Language/implementation is secondary to the ideas expressed
Names or describes a theoretical concept, ontological structure, or recursive process

exclusion_criteria.condition: Has a user-facing interface (GUI, CLI product, web app)
Generates revenue or has a revenue_model
Orchestrates other repos or manages agent behavior
Is primarily documentation or essays

ORGAN-I.reservoir_hosting_note: Theoria hosts cross-organ knowledge reservoirs per PROOF-reservoir-placement v2. Knowledge federation is a form of knowledge production.

boundary_tests.question: Does it produce a conceptual framework consumed by other organs?
Does it have end-users who interact with it directly?
Could it exist as a paper or specification without code?

sibling_organs.ORGAN-II: If the theory becomes an interactive/generative artwork, the art derivative belongs in II
sibling_organs.ORGAN-III: If the theory becomes a product, the product belongs in III

ORGAN-II.name: Poiesis
ORGAN-II.domain_boundary: Generative art, performance systems, creative coding, experiential design. Transforms ORGAN-I theory into interactive or aesthetic artifacts. Art that can be experienced.
inclusion_criteria: Derives from or applies ORGAN-I theory in a creative or artistic context
Produces visual, auditory, interactive, or experiential artifacts
Primary purpose is aesthetic, expressive, or experiential — not commercial
May have a user-facing interface for experiencing the art
Names or describes an artistic concept, creative system, or generative process

exclusion_criteria.condition: Generates revenue as a product
Is a pure framework with no artistic output
Orchestrates other repos
Is documentation about art rather than art itself

boundary_tests.question: Does it produce an artistic or experiential artifact?
Is its primary purpose commercial revenue generation?
Does it derive from or apply theoretical frameworks from ORGAN-I?
Could it be exhibited, performed, or experienced as art?

sibling_organs.ORGAN-I: Pure theory without artistic materialization belongs in I
sibling_organs.ORGAN-III: If the artwork becomes a commercial product (SaaS, marketplace), the product belongs in III

ORGAN-III.name: Ergon
ORGAN-III.domain_boundary: Commercial products, SaaS tools, developer utilities, B2B/B2C applications. Anything with a revenue model or intended for market deployment. The factory floor.
inclusion_criteria: Has or intends to have a revenue_model (subscription, freemium, one-time, etc.)
Is designed for end-users as a product or service
Has or will have deployment infrastructure (hosting, CI/CD, monitoring)
Names or describes a product, tool, utility, or commercial service
Targets a specific market segment or solves a specific customer problem

exclusion_criteria.condition: Is a pure theoretical framework with no product intent
Is primarily an artistic expression
Orchestrates the system rather than serving customers
Is documentation or public process writing
Revenue model is 'none' or 'internal' with no customer-facing aspect

boundary_tests.question: Does it have a revenue model or clear path to one?
Does it serve end-users or customers?
Is it deployed or deployable as a standalone service?

sibling_organs.ORGAN-I: Internal-use-only engines without customer-facing aspects belong in I
sibling_organs.ORGAN-II: Art projects without commercial intent belong in II
sibling_organs.ORGAN-VI: Community features that serve ORGAN-III products feed back via event edges, not by moving repos

ORGAN-IV.name: Taxis
ORGAN-IV.domain_boundary: Orchestration, governance tooling, AI agent management, workflow routing, system coordination. The conductor layer — directs other organs but does not contain their domain logic.
inclusion_criteria: Orchestrates, routes, or coordinates work across other organs
Manages AI agent behavior, skills, or interaction patterns
Provides governance tooling (not governance data — that's META)
Contains workflow definitions, routing rules, or coordination logic
May reference any organ but does not duplicate their domain logic

exclusion_criteria.condition: Contains foundational theory that other organs consume
Is a product serving external customers
Holds the system registry, schemas, or governance data
Is documentation or discourse about orchestration

boundary_tests.question: Does it coordinate or route work across organs?
Does it contain domain logic belonging to I, II, or III?
Would removing it break coordination without losing domain capability?

sibling_organs.META-ORGANVM: Governance data (registry, schemas, rules) belongs in META; governance tooling (agents, routing) belongs in IV
sibling_organs.ORGAN-I: Recursive engines are theory (I); agents that use those engines for orchestration belong in IV

ORGAN-V.name: Logos
ORGAN-V.domain_boundary: Public discourse, essays, building in public, editorial content, analytics about the system. The observatory — reads from many organs, writes to its own domain only.
inclusion_criteria: Produces written content for public consumption (essays, blog posts, documentation about the system)
Observes and documents the system for external audiences
Hosts editorial infrastructure (content management, publishing pipelines)
Names or describes a publication, essay collection, or editorial system

exclusion_criteria.condition: Is internal governance documentation
Is a product that generates revenue from content
Distributes content to external platforms
Is community discussion infrastructure

boundary_tests.question: Does it produce content for public consumption?
Does it only write to its own domain (no cross-organ produces)?
Is the content about the system or its ideas, not the system itself?

sibling_organs.ORGAN-VII: V produces content; VII distributes it to external platforms
sibling_organs.META-ORGANVM: Internal governance docs and SOPs belong in META/praxis-perpetua, not V

ORGAN-VI.name: Koinonia
ORGAN-VI.domain_boundary: Community infrastructure — salons, reading groups, learning cohorts, discussion forums, community taxonomy. Receives events from products (III) and theory (I) to build community around them.
inclusion_criteria: Hosts or manages community interaction infrastructure
Receives product.release or product.milestone events from other organs
Manages community taxonomy, events, or membership
Names or describes a community space, learning group, or social gathering

exclusion_criteria.condition: Is a product that monetizes community features
Distributes content to social platforms
Produces original essays or publications
Is internal team coordination

boundary_tests.question: Does it serve a community of people around the system's products or ideas?
Does it receive events from other organs rather than originating content?
Is community building its primary purpose?

sibling_organs.ORGAN-III: Products feed community via events; community features inside products stay in III
sibling_organs.ORGAN-VII: VI is the community; VII announces to external platforms

ORGAN-VII.name: Kerygma
ORGAN-VII.domain_boundary: POSSE distribution, social automation, announcement pipelines, syndication. Pure consumer — takes content from other organs and distributes it to external platforms. Generates no original domain content.
inclusion_criteria: Distributes content from other organs to external platforms (social media, newsletters, etc.)
Manages POSSE (Publish on Own Site, Syndicate Elsewhere) pipelines
Contains syndication profiles, distribution rules, or platform connectors
Names or describes a distribution channel, announcement system, or syndication pipeline

exclusion_criteria.condition: Produces original content
Manages community interactions
Is a product that monetizes distribution
Orchestrates system-internal workflows

boundary_tests.question: Is it a pure consumer that generates no original domain content?
Does it distribute content to external platforms?
Does it have produces edges in seed.yaml?

sibling_organs.ORGAN-V: V produces content; VII syndicates it externally
sibling_organs.ORGAN-VI: VI is community interaction; VII is one-way broadcasting

META-ORGANVM.name: Meta
META-ORGANVM.domain_boundary: The structural substrate — system registry, JSON schemas, core engine, governance corpus, process SOPs, system dashboard, MCP server, ingestion pipeline. Changes here propagate system-wide. Infrastructure that makes the organ system possible.
inclusion_criteria: Holds system-wide data (registry, schemas, governance rules)
Provides tooling that operates across all organs (engine, dashboard, MCP server)
Contains process governance documentation (SOPs, standards, templates)
Is infrastructure required for the organ system to function
Names or describes a meta-system component, schema, or governance artifact

exclusion_criteria.condition: Is an orchestration agent or routing system
Is a product serving external customers
Is public-facing content or essays
Is foundational theory consumed by other organs
Accumulated experience or memory corpus (non-constitutional knowledge)
exclusion_criteria.rationale: Genome ≠ memory. Constitutional Map §1 enumerates Meta's function: doctrine, registry, schema, migration law. Memory is Theoria's domain per PROOF-reservoir-placement v2.

boundary_tests.question: Would the organ system break without this component?
Does it operate across all organs, not just one?
Is it infrastructure rather than product?

sibling_organs.ORGAN-IV: IV orchestrates agents and workflows; META holds the data and tooling they operate on
sibling_organs.ORGAN-I: Theory engines that are consumed by II/III belong in I; the engine that manages the system belongs in META
